comment_text.snp5.5:3.4: Niddesa treats jāti only as “rebirth” and does not connect it with pabhava in the sense of “origin”. Commentary glosses with jātikāraṇa, which at SN 3.24:4.4 glosses jātipaccaya , where it must mean “by reason of birth”.
comment_text.snp5.5:7.4: Taking nivesana here as “dogma”, the same sense as in the Aṭṭhakavagga. Otherwise it might just mean “strong attachment”.
comment_text.snp5.5:10.3: The expression taṁ taṁ namassāmi also occurs at Snp 5.6:3.3 and Ja 159:1.3. In each case the context is similar: it is an address in second person, followed by a wish or aspiration. The commentary explains the phrase either as tasmā taṁ (“therefore I bow to you”) or as taṁ evarūpaṁ (“I bow to you of such a kind”). The Niddesa ignores the doubled pronoun, which implies it did not give it any special significance (Cnd 8:93.1). I take it as emphatic: “I bow especially to you”.

root_text.snp5.5:4.6: Tathā hi te vidito esa dhammo”.
root_text.snp5.5:9.4: Tathā hi te vidito esa dhammo.
root_text.snp5.5:12.1: Vidvā ca yo vedagū naro idha,

translation_text.snp5.5:1.1: “I ask you, Blessed One; please tell me this,”
translation_text.snp5.5:1.3: “for I think you are a knowledge master, evolved.
translation_text.snp5.5:1.4: Where do all these sufferings come from,
translation_text.snp5.5:1.5: in all their countless forms in the world?”
translation_text.snp5.5:2.1: “You have rightly asked me of the origin of suffering,”
translation_text.snp5.5:2.3: “I shall tell you as I understand it.
translation_text.snp5.5:2.4: Attachment is the source of suffering
translation_text.snp5.5:2.5: in all its countless forms in the world.
translation_text.snp5.5:3.1: When an ignorant person builds up attachments,
translation_text.snp5.5:3.2: that dullard returns to suffering again and again.
translation_text.snp5.5:3.3: So let one who understands <j>not build up attachments,
translation_text.snp5.5:3.4: contemplating the inception of suffering and rebirth.”
translation_text.snp5.5:4.1: “Whatever I asked you have explained to me.
translation_text.snp5.5:4.2: I ask you once more, please tell me this:
translation_text.snp5.5:4.3: How do the attentive cross the flood
translation_text.snp5.5:4.4: of rebirth, old age, sorrow, and lamenting?
translation_text.snp5.5:4.6: for truly you understand this matter.”
translation_text.snp5.5:5.1: “I shall extol a teaching to you,”
translation_text.snp5.5:5.3: “that is apparent in the present, <j>not relying on tradition.
translation_text.snp5.5:5.4: Having understood it, one who lives mindfully
translation_text.snp5.5:5.5: may cross over clinging in the world.”
translation_text.snp5.5:6.3: having understood which, one who lives mindfully
translation_text.snp5.5:6.4: may cross over clinging in the world.”
translation_text.snp5.5:7.5: having cast aside consciousness, don’t continue in existence.
translation_text.snp5.5:8.1: A mendicant who wanders meditating like this,
translation_text.snp5.5:8.2: diligent and mindful, calling nothing their own,
translation_text.snp5.5:8.3: would, being wise, give up the suffering
translation_text.snp5.5:8.4: of rebirth, old age, sorrow and lamenting right here.”
translation_text.snp5.5:9.1: “I rejoice in the words of the great seer!
translation_text.snp5.5:9.2: You have expounded non-attachment well, Gotama.
translation_text.snp5.5:9.3: Clearly the Buddha has given up suffering,
translation_text.snp5.5:9.4: for truly you understand this matter.
translation_text.snp5.5:10.3: Having met, I bow especially to you, <j>O spiritual giant;
translation_text.snp5.5:10.4: hopefully the Buddha may regularly instruct me.”
translation_text.snp5.5:11.1: “Any brahmin recognized as a knowledge master,
translation_text.snp5.5:11.2: who has nothing, unattached to sensual life,
translation_text.snp5.5:11.4: crossed to the far shore, kind, wishless.
translation_text.snp5.5:12.1: And a wise person here, a knowledge master,
translation_text.snp5.5:12.2: having untied the bond to life after life,
translation_text.snp5.5:12.3: free of craving, untroubled, with no need for hope,
translation_text.snp5.5:12.4: has crossed over rebirth and old age, I declare.”
